codeReview.summary: The feature adds substantial local coverage and a useful correlator update, but the live proof does not satisfy several mandatory acceptance criteria. The stage-3 forge script treats required live assertions as log-only/best-effort checks, and the wrapper script can still report success when the correlator never produces the required proof output.

issues.description: The live proof only records whether `RiskGateway.QuoteVerified` appeared and never requires it. Per the handoff, the actual borrow receipt emitted `CrossVMDivergence` because the PVM path failed with StackUnderflow, but the script still completed successfully. That means the feature did not prove the required live `QuoteVerified`/PVM-verification behavior from step 2 and `expectedBehavior["PVM verification during borrow"]`.

Stage 3 explicitly submits `XcmInbox.receiveReceipt()` using the simulated liquidation correlation ID instead of the real on-chain liquidation ID. The committed proof artifact shows `liquidateCorrelationId` (`0xca4a...`) and `receiptCorrelationId` (`0xf393...`) are different, so the live run never proved that the receipt closes the same bilateral correlation chain produced by the actual liquidation.

The feature requirement says to call `XcmInbox.receiveReceipt()` a second time and verify a `DuplicateCorrelationId` revert on testnet. Instead, the script replaced that step with a read-only `hasProcessed()` check and then records the duplicate as proven. `hasProcessed()==true` only shows the first receipt was stored; it does not verify the second live submission actually reverts.

The live liquidation proof only logs whether `XcmLiquidationNotifier.LiquidationNotified` was emitted and does not require it. The committed artifact records `xcmLiquidationNotified: false` and `xcmNotifyAttemptedButFailed: true`, so the feature never proved the required step 5c/5d behavior ('XCM with SetTopic sent' and `LiquidationNotified` emitted) on the canonical deployment.

The wrapper makes the event-correlator step non-fatal by piping `npx ts-node scripts/event-correlator.ts --historical` through `tee ... || true`. As a result, the script can declare completion without the required correlator output. In the reviewed tree there is no committed `deployments/bilateral-correlator-output.json`, and the handoff explicitly says the correlator was not actually run against testnet, so step 9 / verification step 'Correlator JSON output' is unfulfilled.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `deployment-worker` skill is stale relative to the repo's M11 conventions. It still instructs workers to use Hardhat compile/deploy/smoke/verify flows and `npm test`, while mission state and repo services now define Foundry/forge as the canonical path. That mismatch encourages deviations even when workers are marked as having followed procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/skills/deployment-worker/SKILL.md:27-49,62-64` prescribes `npx hardhat compile`, multiple `npx hardhat run ...` deployment/smoke commands, and `npm test`; `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/AGENTS.md:27-29,73-77` and `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/services.yaml` define Foundry (`forge build`, `forge test`, `forge script`) as the repo-standard toolchain.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state does not capture an important proofing pitfall: log-derived values collected during `forge script --broadcast` can come from the simulation block rather than the final on-chain execution. For block-derived IDs like `correlationId`, that can invalidate proof artifacts unless receipts are re-fetched after broadcast.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/library/environment.md` documents required M11 deployment flags, but not this simulation-vs-chain mismatch; `/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/script/BilateralProof.s.sol:366-368` warns that the script is using a SIMULATED liquidation correlation ID; and `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/handoffs/2026-03-19T16-17-59-728Z__bilateral-async-proof__d38ae211-9792-475b-ad54-429c140387a6.json:12` notes that the simulation correlation IDs differed from the actual on-chain values.

summary: Reviewed the bilateral-async-proof handoff, transcript skeleton, commit 324c8cc3d1e16becd0e25572ef194e10a6d2f4a4, and the changed proof/test/correlator files. Result: fail. The local tests are comprehensive, but the live proof still reports success even though it never proved QuoteVerified/PVM verification, never proved the live XCM notifier path, used a simulated receipt correlation ID instead of the liquidation's on-chain ID, skipped the required duplicate-receipt revert, and allowed the correlator step to be silently skipped.
